zh.lifeSummary: 當Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham於1537年出生在英國素福爾可斯頓哈姆 阿斯帕爾時，他的父親John Packard45歲，母親Lady Christine Joan Byrde45歲。他於大約1556年與Joan Avis在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1604年11月7日在英國素福爾可可登哈姆享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham于1537年出生在英国素福爾可斯頓哈姆 阿斯帕爾时，他的父亲John Packard45岁，母亲Lady Christine Joan Byrde45岁。他于大约1556年与Joan Avis在英国英格兰素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1604年11月7日在英国素福爾可可登哈姆享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Packard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English pak(e) ‘pack, bundle’ + the Anglo-Norman French pejorative suffix -ard, probably a derogatory occupational name for a peddler.
English: pejorative derivative of the Middle English personal name   Pack  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Packert, Päckert, from ancient Germanic personal names formed with a word meaning ‘battle’ or ‘to fight’; or a variant of Packer 2 (with excrescent -t).

variants.name: Pack
meanings: English (Kent and Devon): variant of Pask , derived from Middle English Pake ‘Easter, Passover’.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a wholesale trader, from Middle Low German packe, German Pack ‘bundle, package’ (see Packer ). Compare Pak .
Dutch: variant, archaic or Americanized, of   Pak  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Third
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from one or more of various minor places so called because they were at one time farms divided among three tenants.

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Stonham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Stoneham  .

variants.name: Stoneham
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from North and South Stoneham (Hampshire), Stonham Aspal (Suffolk), Little Stonham (Suffolk), or Stoneham Farms in South Malling (Sussex). The Hampshire and Suffolk placenames derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + hām ‘village, homestead’, while the Sussex placename derives from Old English stān + hamm ‘water meadow’.

nameMeanings.name: Moses
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, the English form of the name of the patriarch (Moshe in Hebrew) who led the Israelites out of Egypt (Exodus 4). His name is thought to be of Egyptian origin, most probably from the same root as that found in the second element of names such as Tutmosis and Rameses, where it means ‘born of (a certain god)’. Various Hebrew etymologies have been proposed, beginning with the biblical ‘saved (from the water)’ (Exodus 2:10), but none is convincing. It is now mainly a Jewish name, although until the mid-20th century it also enjoyed considerable popularity among Christians in England, especially among Puritans and Nonconformists.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham was born in 1537, in Stonham Aspal, Suffolk, England, his father, John Packard, was 45 and his mother, Lady Christine Joan Byrde, was 45. He married Joan Avis about 1556, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 7 November 1604, in Coddenham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham est né en 1537, à Stonham Aspal, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Packard, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Lady Christine Joan Byrde, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Joan Avis environ 1556, à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 7 novembre 1604, à Coddenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans.

Lui sposato Joan Avis circa 1556, nel Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonhamが1537年にイギリススフォルクストンハム アスプアルで生まれた時，父John Packardは45歳，母Lady Christine Joan Byrdeは45歳であった。約1556年にイギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハムでJoan Avisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1604年11月7日にイギリススフォルクコッドエンハムで67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham(이)가 1537년 영국 수폴크 스토함 아스팔에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Packard(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Christine Joan Byrde(은)는 45세였다. 그는 대략 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 Joan Avis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1604년 11월 7일 영국 수폴크 코데함에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham nasceu em 1537, em Stonham Aspal, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Packard, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Lady Christine Joan Byrde, tinha 45 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Avis aproximadamente 1556, em Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de novembro de 1604, em Coddenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham родился в 1537 г. в местности стонам аспаль, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John Packard было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Christine Joan Byrde, 45. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Avis около 1556 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 7 ноября 1604 г. в местности кодденам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham nació en 1537, en Stonham Aspal, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Packard, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Lady Christine Joan Byrde, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Joan Avis aproximadamente 1556, en Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 7 de noviembre de 1604, en Coddenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años.
